title: Claude Cowork setup: the only 2 files you need
summary: Claude Cowork is a system that uses two plain text files (CLAUDE.md and MEMORY.md) to maintain persistent context and instructions across sessions. CLAUDE.md contains routing rules and mode-specific instructions, while MEMORY.md preserves project state and decisions between sessions, enabling the AI to recall relevant information like visa requirements from prior work.
writeup: A short walkthrough of building a personal AI second brain inside Claude using only two Markdown files: claude.md as a session instruction manual and memory.md as a persistent project and decision store. The setup lets Claude automatically load context-specific workstations and recall prior decisions across sessions. The concrete example is a Taiwan trip where Claude reads the travel workstation, the instruction file, and the memory file, then surfaces a visa reminder carried over from a previous session.
key_points: Claude reads claude.md at the start of every session, making it an instruction manual that can include routing maps directing the model to load specific workstations based on context (for example, a travel workstation whenever trip planning begins).

memory.md stores active projects and past decisions so the model retains critical information between sessions, such as remembering a specific visa application that was required on a previous trip to Taiwan.

The entire system requires only two files inside a single folder, with no external tools or integrations beyond standard Markdown documents saved within a Claude project (called a Cowork OS folder here).

chapters.title: Memory in Action: Taiwan Trip Example
